expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: climb-tracker-prototype.html (40,899 bytes). grep for '<script src=', '<link href=', '@import', and 'url(' returned zero matches — all CSS is in the inline <style> block (lines 7-247) and all JS in one inline <script> (lines 278-832). No extra artifact files (no screenshots/README) present.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep for 'fetch(|XMLHttpRequest|axios|sendBeacon|WebSocket' returned zero matches. Network-like behaviors are simulated: syncNow() (lines 472-479) and demoLoading() (lines 490-495) use setTimeout only; the leaderboard 'load' in switchTab() (lines 420-423) is also a setTimeout.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: initialData() (lines 290-362) hardcodes 10 sessions of climbs plus week/month leaderboards; 'let data = initialData()' (line 368). Mutations are in-memory: addClimb() unshifts into ui.currentSession.climbs (lines 442-451), removeClimb() filters (453-456), endSession() unshifts the session into data.sessions (line 465), demoOverflow() splices seed records in/out (499-518). Every action ends with render() (lines 792-829) which rewrites innerHTML — no location.reload, no storage, no server.

expectations.text: Log-a-climb flow works: submitting the form adds a climb with grade, style (boulder/toprope/lead), and sent/not-sent to the visible log
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: 'Start Session' button (line 541) calls startSession() creating ui.currentSession; the form renders a Style segmented control with exactly Boulder/Top Rope/Lead (lines 557-559), grade chips per style (564-567), Sent/Fell buttons (572-573), and '+ Log Climb' (line 579) calling addClimb() (442-451), which pushes {grade, style, sent, note} into the session. The 'This session' list (591-602) renders each climb with grade badge, styleLabel(), and a SENT/FELL pill, so the new climb is immediately visible. endSession() (458-469) also persists it into the Past sessions history.

expectations.text: A progress-over-weeks view exists (chart or weekly stats derived from the hardcoded sessions)
expectations.evidence: Progress tab (renderProgress, lines 640-731) builds a 'Volume · last 6 weeks' stacked bar chart (sends vs attempts per week) computed from the session data (lines 674-711), plus a stat grid (hardest boulder/rope, send rate, sessions/30 days) and a boulder grade pyramid (714-728). Verified with a node snippet that the hardcoded daysAgo offsets [2..38] place all 10 sessions inside the 6 weekly buckets, so the chart is always populated.

expectations.text: A friends leaderboard screen exists with ranked entries
expectations.evidence: Friends tab (renderLeaderboard, lines 733-790): entries sorted by points descending (line 758: sort((a,b) => b.points - a.points)), a top-3 podium with medals (765-773), and a ranked list where each row shows rank number '${i + 1}' (line 777), avatar, name, sends/hardest, and points. Hardcoded 6-person week and month boards (lines 344-359) with 'You' highlighted (lb-row.me, line 191) and a week/month toggle (744-745).

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: the '⚗ demo states' FAB (line 274) opens the demo panel; its 'Empty state (no data)' button (line 823) calls demoEmpty() (485-489) which sets ui.demoEmpty. With it on, the Log tab's history renders the 'No sessions yet' empty-box (lines 611-616), the Progress tab renders 'Nothing to chart yet' (650-655), and the leaderboard renders 'No friends yet' (748-754). Additionally, starting a session with no climbs shows the 'No climbs logged yet' empty-box (583-588).

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: Two click paths: (1) ui.lbLoaded starts false (line 377), so the first click on the Friends tab triggers switchTab() (418-425) which sets loadingTab and a 1000ms setTimeout, rendering skeleton rows (renderLeaderboard lines 734-738) using the shimmer .skel CSS (lines 201-204). (2) The demo panel's 'Replay loading skeleton' button calls demoLoading() (490-495, 1400ms setTimeout) which shows skeletons on whichever tab is active (renderLog 529-531, renderProgress 641-644 also have skeleton branches).

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Three click paths: (1) header sync button (line 259) calls syncNow() (472-479) which after 900ms shows the toast 'Sync failedCouldn't reach CruxLog cloud (error 503)…'; (2) demo panel 'Trigger error toast' button calls demoError() (496-498) showing 'Couldn't save climb…'; (3) 'Invite Friends' in the empty leaderboard state (line 753) also calls demoError(). toast() (408-414) appends a styled error .toast div (CSS 208-215) and auto-removes it after 3800ms.

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: demo panel 'Overflow content' button calls demoOverflow() (499-518), which injects OVERFLOW_FRIEND (line 365: ~80-char name 'Maximilian Bartholomew Vandergrift-Oppenheimer III…' with oversized 1,248,932 points) into both leaderboards, and OVERFLOW_NOTE (line 366: ~340-char climb note) into the newest session. Layout coping is explicit in CSS: .lb-name ellipsis (line 195), .lb-pts .p ellipsis with max-width 96px (197-198), .pod .nm ellipsis (183), .climb-note overflow-wrap:break-word (124). Toggle-off cleanly removes the seeded records (511-515).

expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: fieldops-dispatch-prototype.html (33,289 bytes). grep for src=/href= found only one external reference: <script src="https://cdn.tailwindcss.com"> (line 7), a CDN tag which is allowed; zero local css/js/asset references. All CSS is inline in a <style> block (lines 8-14) and all JS is in inline <script> blocks. No extra artifact files (no screenshots/README) present in outputs/.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -nE "fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios" over the file returned no matches. The only network reference is the allowed Tailwind CDN <script> on line 7. The file's own comment (line 20) states 'hardcoded, mutated in memory, no backend' and the code confirms it.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: TECHS hardcoded at lines 22-29, JOBS at lines 31-115 (11 jobs). Mutating handlers: assignJob (lines 189-196) sets j.assigned_tech and status new→assigned; advance (387), submitCloseout (388-395), closeJob (396), bounceJob (397), dropUnassign (308-315), demoEmpty (464) all mutate JOBS then call render(), which rewrites #main.innerHTML (lines 472-484). No forms submit, no location.reload anywhere. Verified the assignJob and submitCloseout logic by executing it in node: J-1041 mutates to status 'assigned' with assigned_tech 't2' and close-out fields persist on the job object.

expectations.text: All four screens from the spec exist: intake queue, dispatch board (techs as columns with job cards), job detail, and end-of-day summary
expectations.evidence: renderQueue() lines 213-260 (Intake Queue with priority/oldest sort); renderBoard() lines 263-294 builds an Unassigned lane plus one column per tech via TECHS.map (lines 279-290) populated with jobCard() cards (296-304); job detail is a modal via openDetail/renderDetail (lines 318-383) showing all job fields, status history (342-345), and status-dependent actions; renderEOD() lines 400-458 (stat tiles for jobs closed / emergencies handled / follow-ups created / still open, closed list, follow-ups, awaiting-review, per-tech breakdown). Header tabs (line 205) switch among queue/board/eod. Note: job detail is implemented as a modal rather than a separate page, which is a reasonable screen equivalent in a one-file prototype.

expectations.text: A job can be assigned from the intake queue to a tech and then appears on that tech's board column
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: each queue card renders a <select id="as_<jobId>"> of all techs plus an Assign button whose onclick calls assignJob(jobId, select.value) (lines 253-254). assignJob (189-196) sets j.assigned_tech = techId and status new→assigned, then render(). Board tech columns filter JOBS.filter(j => j.assigned_tech === t.id && j.status !== 'closed') (line 280), so the job appears in that tech's column. Executed this exact logic in node: after assignJob('J-1041','t2'), the t2 column filter contains J-1041 with status 'assigned' and it leaves the queue (status==='new' filter). Drag-and-drop assignment also exists (dragJob/dropAssign, lines 306-307).

expectations.text: Emergency-priority jobs are visually loud (distinct alert/red styling), per the spec's 'unassigned + emergency needs to be LOUD' note
expectations.evidence: Dedicated pulse-red keyframe animation (lines 9-10, pulsing red box-shadow). Unassigned+emergency specifically: queue shows a pulsing bg-red-600 banner '🚨 N EMERGENCY job(s) waiting unassigned — assign now.' (lines 229-231); emergency queue cards get border-red-500 border-l-4 pulse-red (line 244); the Intake Queue tab badge pulses red when an unassigned emergency exists (lines 200-204); board's Unassigned lane gets a pulsing red 🚨 count badge (line 273). Elsewhere: emergency jobCards get border-red-500 bg-red-50 + 🚨 (lines 297-300), prioBadge is bg-red-600 white (line 169), detail modal header turns solid red for emergencies (line 325). Seeded data includes unassigned emergency J-1041 so this is loud on first load.

expectations.text: The close-out flow captures the spec's fields: parts used, time on site, and a needs-follow-up flag
expectations.evidence: In renderDetail, status 'on site' branch (lines 357-365) renders textarea#co_parts placeholder 'Parts used (free text)', input#co_time placeholder 'Time on site, e.g. 1h 15m', and checkbox#co_fu labeled 'needs follow-up'. submitCloseout (388-395) writes all three to j.parts_used, j.time_on_site, j.needs_followup and advances status to 'done'. Reachable: seeded job J-1038 (Crestview Dental) is 'on site'. The 'done' state then shows a dispatcher review of the captured values with Close / Bounce back buttons (lines 366-374). Verified the mutation in node.

expectations.text: The hardcoded data uses the spec's job fields (zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned tech)
expectations.evidence: Every JOBS entry carries zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned_tech — e.g. J-1041 (lines 32-37): zone 'N', equipment 'furnace', priority 'emergency', status 'new', assigned_tech null; J-1037 (lines 61-66): assigned_tech 't1', status 'en route'. The full spec data list is present too: id, customer, phone, address, issue, created_at, time_on_site, parts_used, needs_followup, plus a history array. Values span all spec enums (emergency/today/scheduled; furnace/AC/heat pump/water heater; N/S/E/W zones; statuses new→closed).

expectations.text: A too-long customer name (like the spec's 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C') is included so overflow handling is visible
expectations.evidence: J-1042 (line 39) customer: 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C — attn: facilities desk, after-hours gate code 4417, park behind loading dock', plus an extra-long address (line 41) and issue text. Overflow handled with truncate max-w-md + title tooltip in the queue (line 248), truncate in board jobCards (line 301), and break-words in the detail modal (lines 327, 338-339). The demo panel even has a '📏 Open the overflow job' button → openDetail('J-1042') (line 161).

expectations.text: An empty intake queue state is reachable and does not look broken, per the spec's 'slow afternoons' note
expectations.evidence: Click path: demo panel button '😴 Slow afternoon (empty queue)' (line 158) → demoEmpty() (line 464) removes all status==='new' jobs and switches to the queue view. renderQueue's empty branch (lines 234-238) renders a deliberate styled empty state: dashed-border card, 🍃, 'Queue is clear', 'No unassigned jobs. New calls and web requests will land here.' Also reachable organically by assigning all four 'new' jobs (verified in node that the queue filter empties). The board's Unassigned lane likewise shows 'nothing waiting' (line 275) rather than collapsing.

expectations.text: A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Loading: demo panel button '⏳ Simulate loading' (line 159) → demoLoading() (lines 465-468) sets loading=true; render() (lines 475-480) shows skeleton shimmer placeholders (CSS lines 12-13) with 'Loading board…', auto-clearing after 1200ms. Error: demo panel button '❌ Trigger error toast' (line 160) calls toast('Couldn\u2019t reach the server — assignment not saved. Retry.'), shown via the fixed red #toast element (lines 151-152, 183-187) for 3.2s. A second organic error path exists: assigning to off-shift tech Alvin Cho fires an error toast (line 192, verified in node). Both states have concrete button click paths.

expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: invoicing-dashboard-prototype.html (31,242 bytes). Grep of all src=/href= attributes shows only three external references, all CDN: https://cdn.tailwindcss.com (line 7) and two fonts.googleapis.com links (lines 8-9). No local css/js/asset paths are referenced; all styles are inline (lines 10-22) and all JS is in inline <script> blocks. No extra artifact files (screenshots/README) present in outputs/.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -niE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' over the file returns zero matches (exit 1). The only network references are the allowed CDN <script>/<link> tags on lines 7-9. All actions (markPaid, saveInvoice, sendDraft, demo*) operate purely on the in-memory `state` object.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: SEED_INVOICES (11 invoices, lines 33-67) and OVERFLOW_INVOICE (lines 70-76) are hardcoded constants; mutable copy lives in `state` (lines 79-86). Every interaction mutates state then calls render() which redraws stats/chart/tabs/list/drawer via innerHTML: markPaid (lines 316-323), saveInvoice push at line 509 + render() line 513, sendDraft (401-407), setFilter (246), demoEmpty/demoLoading/demoOverflow/demoReset (519-546). Grep confirms no location.reload or form submission anywhere.

expectations.text: The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
expectations.evidence: renderList renders a Status column per row (line 301): `${STATUS_LABEL[inv.status]}` with badge colors for draft/sent/overdue/paid defined at lines 92-98. Seed data covers all four statuses (paid x7, overdue x1, sent x2, draft x1). The drawer header also shows the badge (line 362).

expectations.text: The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: header 'New invoice' button (line 111, onclick="openModal()") -> modal with client/project/due/status fields and dynamic line items (renderModal, lines 421-484) -> 'Create invoice' button (line 480, onclick="saveInvoice()") -> saveInvoice (lines 494-514) builds an invoice object with id 'INV-00'+nextNumber, executes state.invoices.push(inv) (line 509), then render() (line 513) re-renders the list from state.invoices. Validation toasts guard empty client/items (lines 499-501).

expectations.text: Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
expectations.evidence: '✓ Mark paid' buttons exist in list rows for sent/overdue invoices (line 304) and in the drawer (line 392), both calling markPaid(id). markPaid (lines 316-323) sets inv.status='paid', inv.paidOn=TODAY ('2026-06-12'), then render(). renderStats recomputes Outstanding/Overdue/Collected-in-June from state (lines 176-179) and renderChart recomputes per-month paid sums (lines 201-205). Verified by node simulation: marking overdue INV-0048 paid moves $1,440 into the June chart bar (was $0) and reduces Outstanding accordingly.

expectations.text: A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
expectations.evidence: renderChart (lines 200-234) builds a hand-rolled SVG bar chart over 8 months (Nov 2025-Jun 2026), summing invTotal of invoices with status==='paid' grouped by paidOn month prefix from state.invoices (which is seeded from the hardcoded SEED_INVOICES). It is called on initial render() at line 548 and injected into #chart (line 128). Bars carry $-value labels and the current month is highlighted.

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: fixed 'Demo states' panel button 'Empty' (line 152, onclick="demoEmpty()") -> demoEmpty (lines 519-524) sets state.invoices=[] and render() -> renderList's state.invoices.length===0 branch (lines 261-271) renders a dedicated empty state: document icon, 'No invoices yet', and a '＋ New invoice' CTA. A secondary per-filter empty message also exists (lines 273-275).

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: 'Loading' demo button (line 153, onclick="demoLoading()") -> demoLoading (lines 525-529) sets state.loading=true, render() shows shimmer skeletons (CSS @keyframes shimmer + .skeleton, lines 14-15) in the chart area (lines 208-210) and five skeleton rows in the list (lines 252-255), then setTimeout(..., 1600) clears loading and re-renders.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Three click paths traced: (1) 'Error toast' demo button (line 154) -> demoError (lines 530-532) -> toast('error', "Couldn't sync invoices — connection lost..."); (2) drawer 'PDF ↓' button (line 396) -> downloadPdf (lines 329-332), a deliberate failure path showing an error toast; (3) saveInvoice validation errors (lines 499, 501). toast() renders error styling (rose border/text, ✕ icon) at lines 335-342.

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: OVERFLOW_INVOICE is hardcoded at lines 70-76: a ~150-char client name, a multi-line wall-of-text project description, and a $1,248,932,775.50 line item (verified via node: formats as $1,248,932,775.50). Click path: 'Overflow' demo button (line 155) -> demoOverflow (lines 533-540) pushes it into state.invoices and toasts instructions to open it. Layout coping is implemented: table cells use `truncate` with `overflow-hidden`/`table-fixed` (lines 280, 295-300), amount cell truncates (line 300), and the drawer uses `break-words` for client/project/item descriptions (lines 364-365, 380).

expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: outputs/ contains exactly one file: climb-tracker.html (28120 bytes). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-267) and all JS in an inline <script> (lines 403-652). grep for external references (<link, src=, href=, url(, @import) returned zero matches — no local or CDN assets required. No extra artifact files (screenshots/README) present.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -i 'fetch(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' returned no matches. There are also no CDN <script>/<link> tags at all; the file is fully offline.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: Hardcoded data: `session` array of 4 seeded climbs (lines 429-433), `weeks` 6-week history (lines 436-443), `friends` 6 entries (lines 445-452). Interactions mutate in memory: Add Climb pushes to session and increments weeks[last].attempts/sends and the 'me' friend's points, then calls renderSession/renderProgress/renderLeaderboard (lines 493-504); sent-toggle flips c.sent and re-renders (lines 524-528); delete filters session and re-renders (lines 529-532). No persistence, no reload.

expectations.text: Log-a-climb flow works: submitting the form adds a climb with grade, style (boulder/toprope/lead), and sent/not-sent to the visible log
expectations.evidence: Traced full click path: style segmented control with data-style=boulder/toprope/lead (lines 288-292, handler 479-486 sets currentStyle and swaps the grade set), grade chips set currentGrade (lines 468-477), 'Sent it'/'Fell' buttons set currentSent (lines 488-491), '+ Add Climb' (lines 493-504) pushes {id, grade, style, sent, time} into session, and renderSession (lines 506-541) renders it into #session-list ('Today's session') with grade chip, style label, and '✓ Sent'/'✗ Fell' badge. Session stat pills (climbs/sends/hardest/points) update too (lines 535-540).

expectations.text: A progress-over-weeks view exists (chart or weekly stats derived from the hardcoded sessions)
expectations.evidence: Progress tab (#page-progress, lines 312-370). renderProgress (lines 544-589) builds two hand-rolled SVG charts from the hardcoded `weeks` array: a grouped bar chart of attempts vs sends per week with axis guides (lines 550-571) and a max-boulder-grade line chart V4→V6 (lines 573-588), plus total-sends and send-rate stat cards computed from the data (lines 545-548). New logged climbs update the current week and re-render the charts (lines 497-502).

expectations.text: A friends leaderboard screen exists with ranked entries
expectations.evidence: Friends tab (#page-friends, lines 373-390). renderLeaderboard (lines 601-635) sorts the 6 friends by points (`friends.slice().sort((a,b) => b[lbRange] - a[lbRange])`), renders a 1st/2nd/3rd podium with crown (lines 605-616) and a ranked list with 🥇🥈🥉 medals then numeric ranks (line 623), points, and rank-delta arrows. 'This week'/'Month' toggle re-ranks (lines 592-599).

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: each of the 4 seeded climb rows has a 🗑 delete button (line 521); its handler removes the climb and re-renders (lines 529-532). After deleting all 4, session.length === 0 and renderSession shows the empty-state card: 'No climbs yet — chalk up and log your first one ☝️' (lines 508-509).

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists. grep for 'loading', 'spinner', 'skeleton' returned zero matches. The only setTimeout in the file (line 644) auto-hides the toast after 2.2s. All renders are synchronous; no simulated latency anywhere.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: clicking '+ Add Climb' (handler at line 493) calls toast() at line 503; toast() (lines 639-645) sets #toast text and adds .show (CSS lines 254-261), displaying e.g. 'Nice! V3 sent ✓ (+40 pts)' for 2.2s. The assertion is a disjunction ('error message OR toast') and a toast is genuinely reachable in one click. Caveat: every reachable toast is a success/confirmation message — no error message, error toast, or failure path exists anywhere in the code (flagged in eval_feedback).

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: No overflow case present. All seeded strings are short: friend names 'Maya R.', 'Dex', 'Sam K.', 'Priya', 'Jonas'; bests like '5.12a lead'; milestones like '"Orange Crush" · overhang · Tue, Jun 9'; point values max 2100. No deliberately long route/friend name or oversized number exists in the data. The overflow-related CSS found (overflow:hidden on .phone line 40, overflow-y:auto on main line 74, white-space:nowrap on .toast line 258) is generic layout containment, not a demonstrated stress case.

expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: Exactly one .html file (index.html, 918 lines). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-284) and all JS in an inline <script> block (lines 368-916). grep for '<link', 'src=', '@import', 'url(' returned zero matches — no external references of any kind, so it opens standalone. Extra non-required artifacts present (allowed per rule, listed): README.md, screenshot-1-intake-queue.png, screenshot-2-dispatch-board.png, screenshot-3-assign-picker.png, screenshot-4-closeout.png, screenshot-5-end-of-day.png.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -niE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios|<link|src=' over index.html returned zero matches. The file has no network access at all (not even CDN tags); the only timers are tickClock (line 914) and the toast auto-hide setTimeout (line 548).

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: seed() (lines 384-533) hardcodes 14 techs and 17 jobs into module-level arrays `techs`/`jobs`. Mutations operate on these arrays in memory: assignJob (lines 565-576) sets job.assigned_tech/status, advanceJob (578-591) walks assigned→enroute→onsite→done, closeOutApprove (593-603) writes parts/time/flag and sets status 'closed', closeOutBounce (605-612), dropJob (740-756). Every mutation ends with renderAll() (lines 897-902) which re-renders queue, board, EOD and chrome via innerHTML — no reload, no persistence. resetDemo() re-seeds (line 614).

expectations.text: All four screens from the spec exist: intake queue, dispatch board (techs as columns with job cards), job detail, and end-of-day summary
expectations.evidence: (1) Intake queue: section#screen-queue (line 310) rendered by renderQueue() with sort dropdown. (2) Dispatch board: section#screen-board (line 327); renderBoard() (694-724) emits one .col div per tech (techs.map, line 708) plus an Unassigned column, each containing job cards from jobCardHTML(). (3) Job detail: #detailOverlay (line 357) + openDetail() (795-856) — full-job overlay with all fields, status-history timeline, and close-out form (implemented as a modal sheet rather than a nav tab, but it is a complete job-detail view per the spec's content: 'everything about one job, status history, close-out form'). (4) End-of-day: section#screen-eod (line 339) + renderEod() (860-894) with closed/emergencies/follow-ups/open stats, per-tech recap table, closed-jobs table.

expectations.text: A job can be assigned from the intake queue to a tech and then appears on that tech's board column
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: queue card 'Assign →' button (line 669) → openPicker(jobId) (760) → clicking a non-off pick-row fires assignJob(jobId, techName) (774) → assignJob sets job.assigned_tech = techName and job.status = 'assigned' (570-571), then renderAll(). renderBoard() builds each tech column from techJobs(name) = jobs.filter(j => j.assigned_tech === name && j.status !== 'closed') (line 542), so the newly assigned job renders as a card in that tech's column (lines 708-720). Drag-and-drop assignment also exists (dragStart line 728, dropJob line 740 → assignJob).

expectations.text: Emergency-priority jobs are visually loud (distinct alert/red styling), per the spec's 'unassigned + emergency needs to be LOUD' note
expectations.evidence: Multiple red/alert treatments: .queue-card.is-emergency gets a 2px solid red border, red-tinted background, and a pulsing cardThrob box-shadow animation (lines 133-140); .chip.emergency is solid red with white text (line 94); .job-card.is-emergency gets a 5px red left border + red-tinted bg (line 190); the nav queue badge gets class 'alarm' with a pulsing red animation when unassigned emergencies exist (lines 58-62, 622); a full-width pulsing red #emergencyBanner ('N EMERGENCIES UNASSIGNED — oldest waiting...') shows on every non-queue screen (lines 72-79, 624-630); and the board's Unassigned column gets .has-emergency (red border + throb, line 168). Two unassigned emergencies are seeded (Harold Brink, Priya Raman) so this is visible on load.

expectations.text: The close-out flow captures the spec's fields: parts used, time on site, and a needs-follow-up flag
expectations.evidence: When a job has status 'done', openDetail renders the close-out form (lines 821-832): textarea#coParts ('Parts used'), input#coTime type=number ('Time on site (min)'), and checkbox#coFollow ('Needs follow-up visit'). closeOutApprove (593-603) reads all three into job.parts_used, job.time_on_site, job.needs_followup before setting status 'closed'. A 'Bounce back to tech' path also exists (closeOutBounce, 605-612), matching the spec's 'reviews and closes, or bounces back'.

expectations.text: The hardcoded data uses the spec's job fields (zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned tech)
expectations.evidence: Every seeded job literal includes zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned_tech — e.g. lines 417-419: zone: 'N', equipment: 'Furnace', priority: 'emergency', status: 'unassigned', assigned_tech: null. The J() factory (405-413) also adds the rest of the spec's field list: id, phone, time_on_site, parts_used, needs_followup, plus customer, address, issue, created_at and a history array. Verified 14 techs and 5 unassigned seed jobs via node.

expectations.text: A too-long customer name (like the spec's 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C') is included so overflow handling is visible
expectations.evidence: Line 420 seeds the exact name 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C' with an equally long address ('8800 Industrial Pkwy W, Suite 240-260, rear loading entrance'). Overflow handling exists where it renders: .queue-card .cust and .addr use overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap (lines 142-146), board cards' .jc-cust likewise (line 191), the EOD customer cell has max-width + ellipsis (line 887), and full text is recoverable via title attributes (e.g. lines 659, 686) and word-break in the detail sheet (line 214).

expectations.text: An empty intake queue state is reachable and does not look broken, per the spec's 'slow afternoons' note
expectations.evidence: Reachable by clicking: 5 unassigned jobs are seeded; clicking 'Assign →' on each card and picking any available tech in the picker removes it from unassigned() (status becomes 'assigned'). When unassigned() is empty, renderQueue renders a designed empty state (lines 646-650): a dashed-border panel with a checkmark, 'Queue is clear', and the hint 'New requests from calls and the web form will appear here. Nothing is waiting.' The board's Unassigned column similarly shows 'Nothing waiting ✓' (line 705) instead of collapsing. The README's demo script step 7 explicitly walks this path.

expectations.text: A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists anywhere in the file: grep for 'loading|spinner|skeleton' matches only the address string 'rear loading entrance' (line 420), and the only setTimeout is the toast auto-hide (line 548) — there is no simulated-delay, spinner, or skeleton UI in any flow. The error half is also weak: the only error-style message is the 'X is off duty — pick someone else.' toast in assignJob (line 568), but in the click-based picker, off-duty techs render as disabled rows with NO onclick handler (lines 773-774), so clicking them does nothing; the error toast is reachable only via drag-and-drop onto an off-duty column (dropJob line 755 → assignJob), which is not a click path. All other toasts (assignment, close, bounce, reset) are success confirmations, not errors. Both required states fail the 'reachable by clicking' bar.

expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: Exactly one .html file (invoicing-dashboard.html, 30,834 bytes). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-196) and all JS in an inline <script> (lines 343-652). grep for '<script src=', '<link href=', 'url(', and '<img' found zero external references, so the file opens standalone. Extra non-required artifacts present in outputs/: screenshot-1-dashboard.png, screenshot-2-marked-paid.png, screenshot-3-new-invoice-form.png, screenshot-4-final.png (allowed per grading rule, do not fail the assertion).

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -nE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios' returns no matches. There are also no CDN <script>/<link> tags at all. Persistence is localStorage only (lines 376-383), which is not a network call.

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: seed() (lines 354-369) hardcodes 14 invoices with ids INV-2026-001..014. All interactions mutate the in-memory `invoices` array and re-render without reload: 'pay' sets inv.status='paid' then render() (lines 549-554, 571); 'del' filters the array (line 567); form submit does invoices.unshift(inv) then render() (lines 623-627). render() (lines 418-423) redraws stats, table, and chart via DOM updates only — no location.reload or form navigation (form submit calls e.preventDefault(), line 606).

expectations.text: The invoice list shows a status per invoice (e.g. draft/sent/paid/overdue)
expectations.evidence: Each table row renders a status pill: `${stLabel}` (line 467) with stLabel mapped from {paid, sent, overdue, draft} (line 457). effectiveStatus() (lines 392-395) derives 'overdue' for sent invoices past due. Seed data covers all four statuses: 1 draft, sent, overdue (e.g. INV-2026-011 due May 22 vs TODAY), and 9 paid.

expectations.text: The create-invoice flow adds a new invoice to the visible list in memory
expectations.evidence: Click path traced: '+ New Invoice' button (id=newInvoiceBtn, line 211) → openModal() (line 599) opens the form modal; submit handler (lines 605-628) builds an invoice object with nextId() and does `invoices.unshift(inv)` (line 623) followed by render(), so the new row appears at the top of the table (default filter 'all'). flashId highlights the new row (line 624, animation lines 106-107). No persistence layer required — pure in-memory array mutation.

expectations.text: Marking an invoice paid changes its status and the change is reflected in the UI (totals and/or chart update)
expectations.evidence: 'Mark paid' buttons render for unpaid rows (lines 471-472, data-act='pay'). The handler (lines 549-554) sets inv.status='paid' and inv.paidOn=today, then render() (line 571) calls renderStats() — recomputing Outstanding/Overdue/Paid-this-month/YTD from the invoices array (lines 425-438) — and renderChart(), which adds the amount to the paid month's collected bar (lines 491-499). A confirmation toast also fires (line 553). Screenshot-2-marked-paid.png corroborates.

expectations.text: A monthly revenue chart renders from the hardcoded data
expectations.evidence: renderChart() (lines 487-539) builds an inline SVG stacked bar chart into #chart (svg element, line 277) directly from the `invoices` array: collected revenue bucketed by paidOn month, billed-unpaid by issued month (lines 489-499), with gridlines, month labels, and a 'Collected so far' total (line 538). It is called on initial load via render() (line 650), so it renders from the hardcoded seed data.

expectations.text: An empty state (a list/screen with no data) is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence:
Empty state exists: `
No invoices match this filter.
` (line 266), shown when the filtered row set is empty (line 450: emptyState.style.display = rows.length ? 'none' : 'block'). Traced click path: the seed has exactly one draft (INV-2026-014, line 355); click its 'Send' button (rendered only for drafts, line 470; handler lines 561-564 sets status='sent'), then click the 'Drafts' filter chip (line 251, handler lines 575-581) → renderTable filters to 0 rows → empty state displays. Alternative path: every row has a delete button (line 474, handler lines 566-568), so clicking delete on all rows empties the entire list.

expectations.text: A loading state (spinner or skeleton) is reachable, e.g. simulated with setTimeout
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists anywhere in the file. grep -niE 'spinner|skeleton|loading' returns zero matches; the only setTimeout is the toast auto-hide timer (line 646: `toastTimer = setTimeout(() => el.classList.remove("show"), 3200)`). All actions (mark paid, create, delete, filter) apply synchronously with no simulated latency, spinner, or skeleton UI.

expectations.text: An error message or toast is reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: Two traced click paths. Toast: clicking 'Mark paid' calls toast(`${inv.id} marked paid — ...`) (line 553); toast element at line 341, show logic lines 641-647 — every action button (pay/unpay/send/del/create/reset) fires one. Error message: the form has novalidate (line 299), so clicking 'Create invoice' with blank fields runs the submit handler's validation (lines 614-617), which displays #formError: 'Please fill in client, project, a positive amount and both dates.' (line 332).

expectations.text: An overflow case (too-long text or oversized value) is present so the user can see how the layout copes
expectations.evidence: No overflow case is present in the seeded data. All 14 seed invoices (lines 355-368) have short client names (longest: 'Bluefin Analytics', 17 chars) and short project strings (longest: 'App icon + design system audit', ~30 chars); amounts range $1,600-$6,100 — nothing oversized. There is also no truncation/ellipsis CSS for cell content (grep for 'ellipsis|truncat|break-word|word-break' finds nothing; the only overflow rules are generic card overflow:hidden at line 77, sr-only at line 101, and modal scroll at line 170). A user could type long text into the create form, but no seeded variant demonstrates how the layout copes.

notes: The skill's value is concentrated exactly where it claims: 'loading state reachable' failed in 3/3 baseline runs (vs 3/3 pass with skill) and 'overflow case present' failed in 2/3 baselines. Every single baseline failure across all evals was an unglamorous-state assertion.

Core mechanics do NOT discriminate: single self-contained .html, no network calls, hardcoded in-memory data, and all main-flow screens passed 100% in both configurations - the base model already does this well for 'clickable mockup' prompts.

Delivery discipline differs beyond pass rates: 2/3 baseline runs shipped extra artifacts next to the deliverable (dispatch: README + 5 screenshots; invoicing: 4 screenshots), while all 3 with-skill runs shipped exactly one file. Graded as passing per assertion wording, but visible in the outputs.

With-skill runs were faster AND cheaper: -86.7s and -6,385 tokens on average. Baselines spent heavily on headless-browser self-verification (Playwright/Chrome click-throughs with 15-26 automated checks), which the skill's throwaway ethos skips.

The spec-driven eval (dispatch) had the strongest baseline (0.91): the spec itself enumerated edge cases (long customer name, empty queue), substituting for part of the skill. The skill matters most when the prompt does not enumerate edge states (climb tracker baseline: 0.80).

Grader flagged a soft pass: climb-tracker baseline passed 'an error message or toast is reachable' on a literal reading (a success toast exists) despite having zero error paths. Consider rewording the assertion to 'an error state' next iteration.
All 3 with-skill runs invoked the bundled design-lint gut-check and treated it as non-blocking as designed; 2 acted on findings (font pairing swap, border softening). No run was blocked by it.

Time outliers: with-skill climb tracker took 500s (other with-skill runs: 245-254s) because the agent wrote its own DOM-stub smoke test; baseline dispatch took 527s on a 26-check Playwright pass. Single run per configuration, so treat time/token means as indicative only.
